pattern: Every zero that immediately follows a one can absorb one operation for each one seen so far, so the total is a single pass counting ones and cashing them in at every fresh 1-to-0 boundary.
hints: An operation is only ever available at a '1' directly followed by a '0' - count how many ones have appeared so far as you scan left to right.

Each time you cross from a '1' into a fresh run of zeros (the previous character was '1' and the current one is '0'), that boundary can absorb exactly one operation for every '1' seen up to that point.
Sum those per-boundary counts in a single left-to-right pass - you never need to actually perform or simulate the moves.

edge_inputs.description: a single 1-to-0 boundary preceded by several ones
several separate boundaries, each accumulating a growing one-count
no ones ever precede a zero, so zero operations are possible
a single character has no boundary at all
all ones leaves no zero to move into
a run of zeros only counts its leading boundary once, not once per zero in the run
a leading run of zeros with no one before it contributes nothing

stress.description: a near-max string biased toward long alternating one/zero runs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    parts = []
    total = 0
    while total < n:
        run_len = rng.randint(1, 50)
        bit = rng.choice('01')
        parts.append(bit * run_len)
        total += run_len
    return [''.join(parts)[:n]]
